title: When Basketballs Fly Sideways
synopsis: In the hour before the big basketball game, Cobble the gnome faces his worst nightmare when a new magical creature arrives who's better at basketball than he is. As chaos erupts in the gymnasium with flying basketballs, exploding scoreboards, and a runaway mascot costume, both Cobble and his rival Pip must learn that some competitions aren't worth winning—and some losses lead to unexpected friendships.

chapters.title: The Calm Before the Catastrophe
chapters.content:
The gymnasium's scoreboard exploded at exactly 6:47 PM, sending sparks raining down like tiny fireworks onto the polished wooden floor.

"Well, that's new," muttered Cobble, the small friendly gnome with his round stone-grey body barely reaching the bottom of the basketball net. His white beard quivered with annoyance as he adjusted his red pointy cap, which had been knocked askew by a falling chunk of scoreboard. The bright black eyes beneath his bushy brows surveyed the smoking wreckage with the kind of stubborn determination that had gotten him into trouble more times than he could count.

You see, dear reader, Cobble had been practicing his three-point shots for exactly forty-seven minutes when the scoreboard decided to have its mechanical breakdown. This was particularly inconvenient because the big game—the annual Whimsical Creatures Basketball Championship—was supposed to start in exactly fifty-three minutes, and Cobble had promised everyone he would finally, finally make at least one basket tonight.

The gymnasium smelled like burnt electronics and floor wax, with just a hint of the cafeteria's mystery meat wafting in from the hallway. Cobble dribbled his basketball—well, attempted to dribble it, since the ball was nearly as tall as he was—and tried to ignore the way the overhead lights flickered ominously every few seconds.

"Okay, Cobble," he said to himself, a habit he'd picked up from spending so much time alone on the court. "Fifty-three minutes to figure out how to actually get the ball through the hoop instead of accidentally launching it into the ceiling tiles."

The last part was reference to an incident from exactly twelve minutes ago, when his enthusiastic shot had created a basketball-sized hole in the ceiling and caused a small avalanche of foam and dust to cascade down like the world's most disappointing snowfall.

illustrations.description: A small friendly gnome with a round stone-grey body, white beard, bright black eyes, and a red pointy cap stands in a school gymnasium with a smoking, sparking scoreboard above, dense tropical jungle vines beginning to creep through the windows, layers of green plants growing along the walls, golden light filtering through the canopy outside, exotic flowers blooming from cracks in the floor, mysterious ancient stone ruins visible through the jungle growth, rich saturated colors, adventure exploration atmosphere
illustrations.alt: Cobble in the gymnasium as the scoreboard explodes with jungle growth beginning

chapters.title: The Rival Arrives
chapters.content:
From Pip's perspective, the smoking gymnasium looked like the perfect place to make a dramatic entrance.

Pip was a pixie—though if you called him small, he'd remind you that he was "vertically efficient"—with wings that shimmered like soap bubbles and a grin that suggested he knew at least three secrets you didn't. He had arrived at Whimsical Creatures Elementary exactly seven days ago, and in those seven days, he had already managed to accidentally turn the library's card catalog into actual cards (playing cards, specifically), make the school's garden gnomes come to life during lunch (they were still arguing about proper mulching techniques), and somehow convince the cafeteria's meatloaf to achieve sentience.

Now, hovering three feet off the gymnasium floor with a basketball balanced casually on one finger like it weighed nothing at all, Pip watched the grumpy gnome below with considerable interest.

"Oh," said Pip, his voice carrying the kind of cheerful confidence that immediately made Cobble's left eye twitch, "are you practicing for tonight's game too?"

Cobble looked up from where he was trying to coax his basketball out from under the bleachers using a mop handle and what appeared to be a cafeteria tray. His white beard bristled with the kind of indignation typically reserved for people who put pineapple on pizza.

"Practicing?" Cobble's voice cracked slightly. "I've been practicing for three months. This is my gymnasium. Well, not mine exactly, but I've been here every day after school since September, and—"

Pip executed a casual backwards somersault in midair and, without even looking, tossed his basketball over his shoulder. It arced gracefully through the air and dropped through the center of the hoop with a satisfying swish.

"Nice gym," Pip said, as if he hadn't just made the kind of shot that Cobble had been attempting unsuccessfully for ninety-two consecutive days. "Mind if I warm up too?"

Cobble's basketball chose that exact moment to roll out from under the bleachers, directly over his foot, and send him tumbling backwards into a cart full of volleyballs that had been innocently minding its own business near the wall.

illustrations.description: A small friendly gnome with a round stone-grey body, white beard, bright black eyes, and a red pointy cap tumbling backwards into a cart of volleyballs while a pixie with shimmering soap-bubble wings hovers above holding a basketball, dense tropical jungle vines covering more of the gymnasium walls, layers of green exotic plants growing from the floor, golden light streaming through jungle canopy that's somehow growing inside, mysterious ancient ruins emerging through the wall, rich saturated colors, adventure exploration atmosphere
illustrations.alt: Cobble falling into volleyballs while Pip the pixie hovers above

chapters.title: The Competition Begins
chapters.content:
Back to Cobble's view of events, which was currently upside-down and involved a volleyball bouncing off his red pointy cap.

"I'm fine!" Cobble announced to no one in particular, though his definition of "fine" apparently included being buried under approximately seventeen volleyballs and what appeared to be a badminton net that had joined the chaos for reasons known only to itself.

As Cobble extracted himself from the sporting goods avalanche, he watched Pip float effortlessly around the court, casually sinking shot after shot. Swish. Swish. Swish. Each successful basket felt like a tiny arrow aimed directly at Cobble's stubborn pride.

You might wonder, dear reader, why a gnome who was clearly struggling with basic basketball fundamentals would feel so territorial about a school gymnasium. The answer, like most answers involving stubborn magical creatures, was complicated and involved a promise Cobble had made to someone who wasn't there anymore.

"So," Cobble said, finally freeing his beard from the badminton net, "you play basketball."

"Oh, this and that," Pip replied modestly, while simultaneously dribbling two basketballs, one with each hand, in a complex pattern that seemed to defy several laws of physics. "I used to play with my cousin Sparklewings back in the Enchanted Forest. Before the whole... incident."

Something in Pip's voice made Cobble pause in his attempt to untangle his cap from a volleyball net. There was a shadow behind the pixie's cheerful expression, the kind of shadow Cobble recognized because he carried one himself.

But before Cobble could ask about it, Pip launched both basketballs simultaneously toward opposite hoops. They arced through the air in perfect synchronization, and for a moment it looked like they would both go in.

Then the ceiling tiles, apparently still traumatized from Cobble's earlier assault, chose that moment to give up entirely. A section about the size of a small car crashed down directly between the two basketballs, sending them ricocheting in directions that basketballs were definitely not supposed to go.

One ball bounced off the falling debris, hit the wall, rebounded off the remaining bit of scoreboard, and somehow ended up lodged in the basketball hoop backwards. The other ball took a more ambitious route, bouncing off three walls, the floor, Cobble's head, and finally coming to rest in the trophy case, where it knocked over the 1987 Regional Spelling Bee Championship trophy with a crash that echoed through the entire school.

illustrations.description: A small friendly gnome with a round stone-grey body, white beard, bright black eyes, and a red pointy cap dodging falling ceiling tiles while basketballs fly in impossible directions around the gymnasium, dense tropical jungle vines now covering most walls, layers of green exotic plants growing up through the floor, golden light filtering through thick jungle canopy overhead, ancient stone ruins visible through the greenery, mysterious adventure exploration atmosphere with rich saturated colors
illustrations.alt: Cobble dodging debris as basketballs bounce chaotically in the jungle-covered gym

chapters.title: Chaos Multiplies
chapters.content:
From Pip's aerial perspective, the gymnasium was beginning to look less like a place for organized sports and more like a disaster zone designed by someone with a twisted sense of humor.

"Did you just—" Pip started to ask, hovering safely above the chaos.

"The ceiling has been wobbly all week!" Cobble protested, though his voice was muffled because he was currently hiding under a gymnastics mat that had apparently declared itself neutral in the ongoing basketball war.

What happened next could only be described as a cascade of increasingly improbable disasters. The basketball lodged backwards in the hoop had created some kind of mystical traffic jam that was preventing the net from working properly. When Pip attempted to dislodge it by gently tapping it with another basketball, the entire hoop assembly began to vibrate.

The vibrating hoop caused the backboard to wobble. The wobbling backboard caused the entire basketball standard to sway. The swaying standard caused a domino effect that somehow activated the automatic scoreboard reset function, which shouldn't have been possible since the scoreboard was mostly a pile of smoking electronics at this point.

But magic, as Pip was beginning to remember, had a way of making the impossible seem routine.

The scoreboard's ghost—or perhaps its very confused computer brain—began cycling through random numbers at increasing speed. 0-0. 47-23. 156-2. 999-888. The electronic buzzer sound, which should have been a pleasant "beep," had apparently been damaged in the explosion and now sounded like a mechanical owl with a head cold.

"HOOOOT-BEEP-HOOOOT-BEEP!"

Each bizarre buzzer sound triggered the gymnasium's ancient ventilation system, which responded by blowing gusts of air strong enough to turn every loose basketball into a flying projectile. And since Cobble's earlier practice session had left approximately fourteen basketballs scattered around the court, the gymnasium suddenly became a very dangerous place to be if you were allergic to getting bonked by sporting equipment.

Pip discovered this when a basketball caught an updraft and smacked him directly in the back, sending him tumbling through the air in a series of uncontrolled loops before he managed to right himself.

"Okay," Pip called down to Cobble, dodging another wind-propelled basketball, "maybe we should work together on this!"

"Now you want to work together?" Cobble shouted back, popping his head out from under the mat just in time to see a basketball bounce off the wall where his head had been a second earlier.

illustrations.description: A small friendly gnome with a round stone-grey body, white beard, bright black eyes, and a red pointy cap hiding under a gymnastics mat while basketballs fly through the air in all directions, dense tropical jungle completely covering the gymnasium walls, layers of green vines hanging from a full jungle canopy ceiling, golden light filtering through exotic plants, ancient stone ruins emerging from the jungle floor, rich saturated colors with adventure exploration atmosphere as chaos reigns
illustrations.alt: Cobble hiding under a mat as basketballs fly everywhere in the jungle-covered gym

chapters.title: The Great Basketball Rebellion
chapters.content:
Cobble's perspective on being pelted by flying basketballs was, unsurprisingly, that it was significantly less fun than it looked.

"This is exactly the kind of thing that happens when show-offs start messing with a perfectly good gymnasium!" Cobble grumbled, army-crawling across the floor toward what he hoped was safety behind the scorer's table.

Unfortunately, the scorer's table had its own problems. Apparently, the ventilation system's hurricane-force winds had activated some kind of ancient magic that Cobble didn't even know existed in the school. The pencils on the scorer's table had begun writing by themselves, scribbling increasingly frantic notes:

"GAME DELAYED DUE TO FLYING SPORTS EQUIPMENT"
"PLEASE REMAIN CALM"
"HAS ANYONE SEEN MY HAT?"
"WHY ARE THE BASKETBALLS ANGRY?"

That last note was actually a very good question. The basketballs did seem angry. Instead of bouncing randomly, they had begun to move with purpose, clustering together in what looked suspiciously like battle formations. A group of five basketballs had formed a defensive line near the free-throw line, while another squadron was executing what could only be described as aerial maneuvers around the ceiling lights.

"Pip!" Cobble called out, momentarily forgetting his annoyance with the pixie. "I think the basketballs are organizing!"

"That's ridiculous!" Pip called back, just as a basketball executed a perfect military turn and headed straight for him. He barely dodged it, his soap-bubble wings shimmering with the effort.

Meanwhile, the gymnasium's PA system crackled to life, though no one had touched it. A voice that sounded suspiciously like the principal's, but with an odd echo, announced: "Attention students and... whatever Cobble is... the gymnasium is currently experiencing technical difficulties. Please do not panic. The basketballs are not actually sentient. Probably."

This announcement had the opposite of its intended effect. The basketballs, apparently offended by the suggestion that they weren't sentient, immediately proved their awareness by forming a perfect circle around Cobble and Pip and beginning to bounce in unison, creating a rhythmic thumping that sounded remarkably like a war drum.

"Okay," Cobble said, his stubborn streak finally giving way to practical concern, "maybe we really should work together."

"I thought you'd never ask," Pip replied, just as the circle of basketballs began to close in.

illustrations.description: A small friendly gnome with a round stone-grey body, white beard, bright black eyes, and a red pointy cap crouching behind a scorer's table with self-writing pencils while basketballs form military formations in the air around him, dense tropical jungle now completely covering the gymnasium with thick vines, layers of exotic green plants creating a canopy ceiling, golden light streaming through the jungle atmosphere, ancient stone ruins emerging everywhere, rich saturated colors with mysterious adventure exploration feel
illustrations.alt: Cobble hiding behind a table with magical writing pencils as basketballs form battle formations

chapters.title: Unlikely Allies
chapters.content:
From Pip's viewpoint, teaming up with a grumpy gnome to fight a rebellion of sporting goods was definitely not how he'd planned to spend his Tuesday evening.

"What's the plan?" Pip asked, hovering just above Cobble's red pointy cap while the circle of basketballs continued their ominous bouncing.

"Plan?" Cobble squeaked. "I was hoping you had a plan! You're the one who can actually make baskets!"

"Well, yes, but you're the one who's been here every day for three months," Pip pointed out, dodging a basketball that had apparently decided to break formation and go rogue. "You must know something about this place that I don't."

Cobble was quiet for a moment, his bright black eyes darting around the increasingly chaotic gymnasium. The truth was, he did know something about this place. Something he hadn't told anyone, because it was the kind of truth that made his throat tight and his eyes watery.

"My grandfather," Cobble said finally, his voice barely audible over the rhythmic basketball thumping, "used to be the janitor here. He taught me to play basketball on this court when I was even smaller than I am now."

Pip landed on the scorer's table next to Cobble, his soap-bubble wings folding behind him. "Used to be?"

"He's... not here anymore," Cobble said, adjusting his cap nervously. "But he made me promise I'd keep practicing until I could make one perfect shot. Just one. He said every gnome needs to accomplish something that seems impossible."

The confession hung in the air between them like a soap bubble, fragile and iridescent.

"My cousin Sparklewings," Pip said quietly, "taught me everything I know about basketball. We used to play in the Enchanted Forest until the developers came and turned our court into a shopping mall. He said sports were about bringing creatures together, not showing off."

For a moment, both the gnome and the pixie sat in companionable silence, surrounded by angry basketballs and the increasingly tropical atmosphere of the gymnasium, which seemed to be growing actual vines now.

"So," Pip said eventually, "what would your grandfather do about rebellious sports equipment?"

Cobble's white beard twitched in what might have been a smile. "Probably something completely ridiculous that somehow worked perfectly."

"Perfect," Pip grinned. "I specialize in ridiculous."

illustrations.description: A small friendly gnome with a round stone-grey body, white beard, bright black eyes, and a red pointy cap sitting next to a pixie with soap-bubble wings on a scorer's table, surrounded by bouncing basketballs in formation, dense tropical jungle completely enveloping the gymnasium with thick green vines, layers of exotic plants creating a full canopy, golden light filtering through mysterious ancient ruins scattered throughout, rich saturated adventure exploration atmosphere
illustrations.alt: Cobble and Pip sitting together surrounded by bouncing basketballs in a jungle gym

chapters.title: The Ridiculous Plan
chapters.content:
Cobble's contribution to their ridiculous plan was based on three months of careful observation and one crucial piece of information: the gymnasium's ancient magic was tied to the original cornerstone, which was located directly under the center of the court.

"So you're saying," Pip clarified, as they prepared to execute their strategy, "that if we can get all the basketballs to bounce on the center circle at exactly the same time, it will reset the gymnasium's magic and everything will go back to normal?"

"Probably," Cobble said with the kind of confidence that suggested he was making up approximately sixty percent of his plan as he went along. "My grandfather always said this place had more magic in it than sense."

The plan, such as it was, required Cobble to use his stubborn gnome determination to stay in the center circle no matter what, while Pip used his flying abilities to herd the rebellious basketballs into position. It was the kind of plan that would either work brilliantly or result in them both being trampled by angry sporting goods.

"Ready?" Pip asked, his wings shimmering with nervous energy.

"Ready," Cobble lied, clutching his original basketball—the one that had started this whole mess—like a stone-grey teddy bear.

What followed was possibly the most chaotic game of magical basketball herding in the history of educational athletics. Pip zoomed around the gymnasium, which now looked more like a jungle temple than a school facility, using a combination of aerial acrobatics and strategic trash-talking to convince the basketballs to follow him.

"Hey, basketballs!" Pip called out, executing a loop-de-loop around the scoreboard's remains. "Bet you can't all bounce in the same place at the same time! You're probably not coordinated enough!"

The basketballs, apparently possessing both sentience and pride, took this as a personal challenge. They began converging on the center circle where Cobble stood, his red pointy cap trembling but his stubborn feet planted firmly on the magical focal point.

As the basketballs gathered in a perfect circle around him, Cobble closed his bright black eyes and thought about his grandfather. About promises kept and shots attempted. About the difference between winning and succeeding.

When he opened his eyes, every basketball in the gymnasium was bouncing in perfect synchronization around him, and the jungle vines covering the walls had begun to glow with soft, golden light.

illustrations.description: A small friendly gnome with a round stone-grey body, white beard, bright black eyes, and a red pointy cap standing bravely in the center circle surrounded by basketballs bouncing in perfect synchronization while a pixie flies overhead, dense tropical jungle covering the gymnasium with glowing golden vines, layers of exotic plants creating a magical canopy, ancient stone ruins glowing with mysterious light, rich saturated colors with adventure exploration atmosphere as magic builds
illustrations.alt: Cobble standing courageously in the center as basketballs bounce in sync around him

chapters.title: The Perfect Shot
chapters.content:
From Pip's aerial view, the magical reset looked like someone had shaken up a snow globe filled with golden light and basketball-shaped confetti.

The synchronized bouncing of seventeen basketballs on the gymnasium's magical cornerstone created a resonance that vibrated through the building's foundation, up through the jungle vines, and out into the evening air like a musical chord played on the world's strangest instrument.

One by one, the rebellious basketballs settled into peaceful, non-sentient stillness. The jungle vines retreated into the walls, leaving behind only the faintest suggestion of tropical magic in the air. The scoreboard reassembled itself with a series of cheerful electronic chirps, displaying a sensible "00:00" instead of random numbers.

Even the ceiling tiles, apparently embarrassed by their earlier dramatic collapse, quietly rebuilt themselves while no one was looking.

"Well," Pip said, landing next to Cobble in the center circle, "that was—"

He was interrupted by the sound of the gymnasium doors opening. The entire Whimsical Creatures Basketball team poured in, along with parents, teachers, and what appeared to be half the magical community of the greater metropolitan area. They stopped in confused amazement at the sight of the perfectly normal, completely unmagical, absolutely ordinary gymnasium.

"Cobble?" called Coach Thistlebottom, a particularly large troll who doubled as the school's librarian. "Why are you standing in the center circle holding a basketball like it's made of gold?"

Cobble looked down at the basketball in his hands—his original basketball, the one he'd been practicing with for three months. Without really thinking about it, he dribbled it once, twice, then took careful aim at the nearest hoop.

The shot wasn't perfect. It hit the rim, bounced up, hit the backboard, bounced back to the rim, and teetered there for what felt like an eternity while the entire gymnasium held its breath.

Then it dropped through the net with the most beautiful swish Cobble had ever heard.

"Finally," Cobble whispered, and felt something tight in his chest let go for the first time in months.

"Nice shot," Pip said quietly. "Want to be on the same team tonight?"

Cobble looked at the pixie—really looked at him—and saw the same shadow he'd recognized earlier, the same loss, the same stubborn hope.

"Yeah," Cobble said, his white beard no longer bristling with annoyance. "I'd like that."

illustrations.description: A small friendly gnome with a round stone-grey body, white beard, bright black eyes, and a red pointy cap making a basketball shot while a pixie hovers nearby, the gymnasium now returned to normal but with subtle hints of jungle magic in the corners, golden light streaming through regular windows, ancient ruins barely visible as decorative elements, rich saturated colors with a peaceful adventure exploration atmosphere as teammates enter
illustrations.alt: Cobble making his perfect shot as the gymnasium returns to normal

chapters.title: After the Game
chapters.content:
The actual basketball game, it turned out, was significantly less eventful than the hour leading up to it.

Cobble and Pip's team won by three points, though neither of them was particularly concerned with the score. They had discovered something more important than winning: the peculiar magic that happens when two creatures who understand loss decide to build something together instead of competing over what they've lost.

After the game, as the gymnasium emptied and the evening janitor began his rounds, Cobble and Pip sat on the bleachers sharing a bag of popcorn that someone had left behind.

"So," Pip said, tossing a kernel into the air and catching it in his mouth, "tomorrow after school?"

"Tomorrow after school," Cobble agreed. "But maybe we start with some basic passing drills before we accidentally activate any ancient magic."

"Where's the fun in that?" Pip grinned, his soap-bubble wings catching the last rays of sunlight streaming through the gymnasium windows.

Cobble adjusted his red pointy cap and smiled back. His grandfather had been right about promises and impossible things. But he'd left out the best part: sometimes the impossible things were even better when you shared them with a friend.

The gymnasium settled into peaceful evening quiet, the kind of silence that holds the echo of laughter and the promise of tomorrow's adventures. In the trophy case, the 1987 Regional Spelling Bee Championship trophy sat in its proper place, completely unaware that it had been knocked over by a rogue basketball just two hours earlier.

And in the center circle, if you looked very carefully in just the right light, you could still see the faintest shimmer of magic—not the chaotic kind that turns basketballs into rebels, but the gentle kind that turns strangers into teammates and practice shots into perfect moments.

As Cobble and Pip left the gymnasium together, neither of them noticed that the scorebaord displayed one final message before going dark: "GAME OVER. FRIENDSHIP WINS."

But then again, dear reader, the best magic often happens when no one's looking.

illustrations.description: A small friendly gnome with a round stone-grey body, white beard, bright black eyes, and a red pointy cap sitting on bleachers next to a pixie with soap-bubble wings, sharing popcorn in a peaceful gymnasium with subtle traces of jungle magic glowing softly in the corners, golden evening light streaming through windows, hints of ancient ruins as decorative elements, rich saturated colors with a warm adventure exploration atmosphere conveying friendship and contentment
illustrations.alt: Cobble and Pip sharing popcorn on the bleachers in the peaceful evening gym

metadata.dedication: For everyone who has ever made a promise to someone who isn't there anymore

coverPrompt.description: A small friendly gnome with a round stone-grey body, white beard, bright black eyes, and a red pointy cap stands in a chaotic school gymnasium with basketballs floating in the air around him, dense tropical jungle growing through the walls, layers of green vines covering the basketball hoops, golden light streaming through the canopy above, exotic plants sprouting from the wooden floor, mysterious ancient ruins visible through the jungle growth, rich saturated colors, adventure exploration atmosphere
coverPrompt.alt: Cobble the gnome in a magical jungle-covered gymnasium with floating basketballs

questions.question: What exploded at the beginning of the story?
questions.explanation: The scoreboard exploded at exactly 6:47 PM, sending sparks raining down onto the gymnasium floor.

questions.question: What kind of creature is Pip?
questions.explanation: Pip is a pixie with wings that shimmer like soap bubbles and a talent for making impossible basketball shots.

questions.question: Why was Cobble practicing basketball every day?
choices: To keep a promise to his grandfather
questions.explanation: Cobble had promised his grandfather, who used to be the school janitor, that he would keep practicing until he could make one perfect shot.

questions.question: What happened to the basketballs during the chaos?
questions.explanation: The basketballs became sentient and began organizing into military formations, bouncing in unison like they were angry.

questions.question: How did Cobble and Pip solve the basketball rebellion?
choices: They got all basketballs to bounce on the center circle
questions.explanation: Their plan involved getting all the basketballs to bounce on the center circle simultaneously to reset the gymnasium's ancient magic.

questions.question: What happened when Cobble finally took his shot after the magic reset?
choices: It bounced around before going in
questions.explanation: Cobble's shot hit the rim, bounced up to the backboard, back to the rim, and teetered there before finally dropping through with a swish.

questions.question: What did Cobble and Pip have in common?
choices: They both were new to the school
questions.explanation: Both Cobble and Pip carried shadows of loss - Cobble's grandfather was gone, and Pip's cousin Sparklewings was no longer part of his life.

questions.question: What was the final message on the scoreboard?
questions.explanation: The scoreboard's final message before going dark was 'GAME OVER. FRIENDSHIP WINS.'
